hypothesis: An equivariant graph neural network trained on the XDIP dataset to predict Fe K-edge XANES spectra directly from 3D local atomic structures will achieve significantly lower prediction error than non-equivariant GNN baselines (GCN, GAT, GIN), and the learned geometric representations will encode sufficient electronic-structural information to simultaneously classify iron oxidation state (Fe²⁺ vs. Fe³⁺) with accuracy exceeding 85%, demonstrating that enforcing rotational and translational symmetry in the model architecture is the critical bottleneck—rather than dataset size—for bridging the structure-to-spectrum gap in iron-containing proteins.
